purpose: Add the supported non-mac standalone Pylon release assets to the existing v0.1.11 release so npm bootstrap can install Linux binaries instead of falling back to source builds.

unsupported_targets.reason: packages/pylon-bootstrap v0.1.11 only resolves darwin and linux targets; Windows remains on the source-build fallback path rather than an npm-bootstrap release-asset path.

builds.builder: local macOS arm64 using zig and cargo-zigbuild
builds.source: temporary detached worktree at tag pylon-v0.1.11
verification: ELF type verified as x86-64 GNU/Linux with GNU/Linux 2.0.0 floor
uploaded asset was fetched by npm bootstrap and runtime-smoked on archlinux
installed pylon --help executed successfully on archlinux
installed pylon-tui --help executed successfully on archlinux
bootstrap pylon init/status/inventory smoke completed on archlinux
builds.build_note: An earlier native Arch x86_64 archive was built and runtime-smoked, but it was superseded by this Zig-built archive to avoid baking in rolling-Arch glibc requirements. The release asset was clobbered with the Zig-built archive and then npm-smoked on Arch.

builds.builder: local macOS arm64 using zig and cargo-zigbuild
builds.source: temporary detached worktree at tag pylon-v0.1.11
verification: ELF type verified as ARM aarch64 GNU/Linux for pylon and pylon-tui
builds.caveat: No Linux arm64 Tailnet host was available, so this target was not runtime-executed. The npm bootstrap supports the asset name and will fetch it on Linux arm64 hosts.

observed: npm bootstrap selected the Linux x86_64 release archive
bootstrap installed pylon and pylon-tui under the isolated install root

release_notes.summary: Release body now lists darwin-arm64, linux-x86_64, and linux-arm64 archives plus checksums, and explicitly notes that Windows is not a v0.1.11 npm-bootstrap target.
